_readme: cot_radio_aprs v2.0 canonical iconset dictionary. Published here for Part 97 transparency — any licensed amateur operator can download this file and decode the 4-char icon codes used in v2.0 packets on ham radio frequencies. IDs are 3-char base-36 (0-9, A-Z) alphanumeric. Additive-only within a major version; existing IDs never change. Removing or renumbering requires major-version bump and invalidates archived traffic.

_notes.id_format: Each icon ID is a 3-character base-36 string (0-9, A-Z). Capacity per iconset = 46,656. Ordering within an iconset is alphabetical by (group, filename) — deterministic so the ID never shifts as the dictionary is extended.
_notes.description_source: Descriptions are derived from filenames with underscores and hyphens replaced by spaces. For common icons this reads naturally (e.g. 'car', 'antelope', 'command post'). Operators may propose plain-English refinements via PR.
_notes.custom_iconsets: Codes X, Y, Z reserved for operator-custom iconsets. Must be published per §7 Part 97 compliance when used on amateur frequencies.
_notes.reserved_letters: Codes not currently assigned: B, C, H, J, K, N, Q, S, T, U, V, W. Available for future canonical iconsets.
